what_it_does: This file is a frequency-ordered aggregate list of named code/design substrates produced by a wizard run: each line pairs an integer count with a component, concept, file, surface, registry, or formal entity.
observations: Many entries are UI surfaces, panels, and registry systems (commandregistry, capabilityregistry, component-registry, etc.)
Substantial formal/mathematical vocabulary: sheaf, determination function, boundary proof, convergence, kleene fixed-point, clifford algebra, coalgebra
Wizard-specific artifacts appear: recipe ids (s-02, s-03, s-06, etc.), viewport outputs, round_queue, wizard_state, wizard_templates
Supabase/MCP/edge-function vocabulary is dense: rls policies, edge functions, rpcs, mcp tools, convai tools
Vault/Obsidian integration is prominent: vault_read, vault_edit, vault_search, vault_frontmatter, obsidian vault agents
Counts range from 287 down to 1, with a very long tail of rare items
File ends with a truncation note
No categories, provenance, or units attached to counts
standouts: 190 stats-update context signals a central runtime concern
Formal substrate layer: sheaf/determination/convergence/boundary-proof treated as first-class components
Mixture of concrete files (app.tsx, tailwind.config.js) and abstract constructs (holonic structure, completion monoid)
Explicit counts for architectural contracts: 13 systems, 6 workflows, 14 registries, 27 mcp tools, 39 rpcs
concerns: Counts lack definition: mentions, occurrences, weighted significance, or file hits?
Many near-duplicate labels with different casing/hyphenation (e.g., 'substrate_ledger' vs 'substrate ledger', 'determination sheaf' vs 'determination-sheaf')
Heterogeneous granularity: single function, whole file, abstract concept, and UI panel all treated as one 'component'
File is truncated, so totals and tail completeness are unknown
No source scope or version, making the counts hard to reproduce
notes: A frequency-weighted, uncategorized inventory of candidate migration substrates from a wizard run, mixing code files, UI surfaces, registries, and formal-math constructs.
questions: What is the exact counting rule used to produce these numbers?
What corpus or file set was scanned for this aggregation?
Why was the file truncated and how much is missing?
Are the formal/mathematical terms extracted from documentation or inferred by the wizard?
How should near-duplicate labels be normalized before fusion?
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to enumerate and weight every relevant code/design substrate so a migration wizard can prioritize, track, and fuse components into a coherent ledger.
novelty: The unusual co-presence of concrete software artifacts (files, panels, hooks) with advanced mathematical formalisms (sheaves, determination functions, clifford algebras) as equally weighted ledger entries.
contribution: A frequency-weighted seed list of candidate migration substrates that a fused system can use to bootstrap a normalized component/substrate ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A truncated, frequency-sorted aggregate list of code, design, and formal-mathematical substrates produced by a migration wizard run.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a weighted inventory of candidate components for migration tracking and ledger construction.
